mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate drops by more than 1 percentage point over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand recovery, producing positive monthly S&P 500 returns through earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of co-expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stabilizing supply chains and manufacturing output, which generates positive weekly equity returns during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where industrial production increases, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers real yield volatility, producing negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of months following CPI MoM readings above 0.3%, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 consistently over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment confirms sustainable employment recovery, driving positive quarterly equity returns via wage-growth and consumption channels.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into positive weekly equity returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter moving average, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in industrial production signal rapid capacity utilization recovery, generating positive monthly equity returns as corporate output aligns with pent-up demand.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where industrial production growth exceeds 1.5%, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in walk-forward evaluation.
